StatusNote: Season shell added from the May 12, 2004 Savannah Morning News reference to Mark Gibbons as St. Andrew's second-year coach.
HistoricalSummary: The 2002-03 girls soccer season is currently a shell in the archive. Mark Gibbons is listed as head coach based on a May 12, 2004 Savannah Morning News recap that identified him as St. Andrew's second-year coach.

SeasonResult: St. Andrew's followed a third-place finish in the 16-team Cavalier Classic with two wins in the Kelme Tournament of Champions, four recovered region wins, an April 29 win over Beaufort Academy that clinched the SCISA Region 1-AA championship, and a run to the SCISA Class A/AA state final.
StatusNote: Archive build in progress. The May 15 state final report listed St. Andrew's at 14-3 after Greenwood Christian won the championship on penalty kicks. The recovered game list does not yet account for every result in that published record.
HistoricalSummary: The Spring 2004 girls soccer archive currently begins with a March 9 match against Frederica Academy and a March 11 region match against Colleton Prep. Frederica won the opener 4-0, then St. Andrew's answered with a 9-0 SCISA 2-AA region win over Colleton Prep.

St. Andrew's then posted consecutive shutouts at the Cavalier Classic at Mount De Sales High School in Macon, beating Dominion Christian 2-0 and First Presbyterian Day 1-0. Jennifer Moesch scored all three Cavalier Classic goals listed in the March 20 report, and Mary Wilkowski made 13 saves across the two wins.

On March 20, St. Andrew's lost 5-0 to Frederica Academy in the Cavalier Classic semifinal, then defeated Fellowship Christian 3-2 in the third-place game. The Lions placed third out of 16 teams, and Jennifer Moesch and Mary Wilkowski were named to the all-tournament team.

St. Andrew's added two March 27 wins in the Kelme Tournament of Champions, defeating The King's Academy 6-0 and Trinity Collegiate 4-1. Jennifer Moesch scored five goals across the two games, while Mary Wilkowski made five saves.

On March 29, St. Andrew's defeated Beaufort Academy 5-0 in SCISA Region 2-AA play. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals, Leighanne Evans scored twice, and Mary Wilkowski made two saves as the Lions improved to 7-2 overall and 2-0 in the region.

St. Andrew's defeated Colleton Prep 5-1 in an April 1 GISA Region 2-AA game. Layne Haggberg scored twice, and Leighanne Evans, Jennifer Moesch, and Rebecca Timms each scored once as St. Andrew's moved to 8-2 overall and 3-0 in region play.

On April 2, St. Andrew's beat Trinity Collegiate 5-1 in a non-region match. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals, Megan Johnson and Grace Wilkowski also scored, and Rebecca Timms, Emily Aimone, and Moesch recorded assists.
On April 6, St. Andrew's played Hilton Head Christian to a 0-0 draw. Mary Wilkowski made 12 saves as St. Andrew's moved to 9-2-1.

On April 29, St. Andrew's defeated Beaufort Academy 5-0. Jennifer Moesch scored three goals, Grace Wilkowski and Becca Timms also scored, and Emily Aimone recorded an assist as St. Andrew's clinched the SCISA Region 1-AA championship.

In the SCISA Class AA/A state tournament, St. Andrew's defeated The King's Academy 8-0 in the quarterfinals and Lowcountry Day School 5-0 in the semifinals to earn a spot in the state championship at Heathwood Hall School.

St. Andrew's finished as the SCISA Class A/AA State Runner-up after Greenwood Christian beat the Saints 2-1 on penalty kicks in the May 15 state final. Jennifer Moesch scored the tying goal with 2:32 left in regulation, and Mary Wilkowski made 10 saves.
HighlightNotes: Jennifer Moesch scored four goals in a 9-0 region win over Colleton Prep.
Jennifer Moesch scored three goals across Cavalier Classic wins over Dominion Christian and First Presbyterian Day.
Jennifer Moesch scored twice and Leighanne Evans scored once in the Cavalier Classic third-place game.
Jennifer Moesch scored three goals in a 4-1 Kelme Tournament of Champions win over Trinity Collegiate.
Jennifer Moesch scored three goals and Leighanne Evans scored twice in a 5-0 region win over Beaufort Academy.
Layne Haggberg scored twice in a 5-1 region win over Colleton Prep.
Jennifer Moesch had three goals and two assists in a 5-1 non-region win over Trinity Collegiate.
Mary Wilkowski made 12 saves in a 0-0 draw with Hilton Head Christian.
St. Andrew's clinched the SCISA Region 1-AA championship with a 5-0 win over Beaufort Academy.
Jennifer Moesch scored four goals in a 5-0 SCISA semifinal win over Lowcountry Day School.
St. Andrew's finished as the SCISA Class A/AA State Runner-up.
Jennifer Moesch and Mary Wilkowski were named to the Cavalier Classic all-tournament team.
